items.title: 14 families keep Pulau Raya's 200-year heritage alive
items.summary: TANAH MERAH: Pulau Raya, one of Kelantan's smallest villages, is home to 14 Chinese families preserving a way of life that has endured for more than two centuries. Village head Wee Thin Kim, 51, said the island, believed to be more than 200 years old, had changed significantly over the decades. He said Pulau Raya was once home to both Malay and Chinese families, but today only the Chinese community remains.

items.title: Samsung cuts US jobs, offers relocations ahead of HQ move
items.summary: SEOUL: Samsung Electronics has cut jobs at its US display, phone and other consumer electronics operations — affecting workers mainly in New Jersey and Texas, according to documents and two people familiar with the matter. The South Korean tech giant said on Sunday in a statement to Reuters that 739 roles in Englewood Cliffs, New Jersey, have been affected by plans by Samsung Electronics America (SEA) — which is focused on consumer electronics and does not include chips — to move its headquarters to Texas. Samsung's decision to shift SEA's headquarters is striking because SEA employees in New Jersey only moved to new offices with much fanfare less than a year ago.

items.title: Syed Saddiq to focus on marriage before deciding on Muda leadership return
items.summary: MUAR: Muar member of parliament Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman has yet to consider reclaiming the leadership of Muda following his recent court acquittal. Instead, the former youth and sports minister said he would focus on the next chapter of his life, which is building a family. Syed Saddiq is engaged to actress and singer Bella Astillah .

items.title: Pesona Metro bags RM247.5mil hospital job
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pesona Metro Holdings Bhd has secured a RM247.5 million contract to build a private hospital in Setia Alam, Selangor. Pesona Metro said its wholly-owned Pesona Metro Sdn Bhd had accepted a Letter of Award from Setia Alam Medical Centre Sdn Bhd (SAMC) for the execution and completion of piling, basement, main building and external works for the project. The proposed development comprises three hospital blocks with supporting facilities and multi-storey car parks, located in Setia Alam, Shah Alam, Selangor.

items.title: Resign if you want to attack unity partners, Anwar tells ministers
items.summary: IPOH: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has warned ministers, deputy ministers and heads of federal agencies to resign if they intend to use their federal positions to attack unity government partners during state election campaigns. Anwar said while political parties aligned at the federal level were free to contest against one another in state elections, those holding federal office must maintain discipline and avoid turning their positions into political platforms. Anwar added that the reminder was conveyed during his discussions yesterday with Deputy Prime Ministers Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof, following the ongoing election campaign in Negri Sembilan.

items.title: KWAP details response to eFishery fallout, RM163.4mil hit by fraud
items.summary: KWAP said it had strengthened its private market investment framework while pursuing avenues to recover its investment. KWAP said its total investment in eFishery amounted to RM163.4 million, representing about 2.51 per cent of the company's total shareholding. KWAP said it has since strengthened its private market investment approach.

items.title: N9 polls: Act now or lose chance forever, says Tok Mat
items.summary: SEREMBAN: The understanding between Barisan Nasional (BN) and Perikatan Nasional (PN) in Negri Sembilan is an important effort to safeguard the future of race and religion, Umno deputy president Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said. He said the initiative was first mooted in 2018 but did not materialise. Mohamad, who is also the Negri Sembilan BN chairman, said the effort must begin now, warning that failing to do so would mean losing the opportunity forever.

items.title: Heavy rain warning issued for KL, Selangor and nine other states until 5pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 19 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds expected to hit several states in the Peninsula, Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan until 5pm today. Similar warnings were also issued for Kuala Lumpur, Putrajaya, Melaka, Selangor (Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat, Hulu Langat and Sepang), Negeri Sembilan (Jelebu, Seremban, Port Dickson, Kuala Pilah, Rembau and Tampin) and Johor (Tangkak, Segamat and Muar). In Sarawak, the areas involved are Kuching, Samarahan, Mukah (Matu, Dalat, and Mukah), Kapit (Song), and Miri (Subis, Telang Usan, and Miri), while in Sabah, it affects the Interior (Kuala Penyu) and Tawau (Tawau and Semporna).

items.title: Pas pitches broad coalition ahead of 16th General Election
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pas has proposed the formation of a broad political coalition involving Barisan Nasional, Perikatan Nasional and parties from Sabah and Sarawak, saying such an alliance would provide greater political stability and strengthen Malay-Muslim unity ahead of the 16th General Election. Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man said the party hoped recent electoral cooperation between BN and PN in Johor and Negri Sembilan would pave the way for a wider political understanding involving Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) and Gabungan Parti Sarawak (GPS). Tuan Ibrahim said Pas believed the Johor election had demonstrated the benefits of electoral cooperation between BN and PN, despite the party failing to win any of the seats it contested.

items.title: Akmal Saleh defends BN-PN understanding, hits back at PH criticism
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh has questioned PKR Youth chief Muhammad Kamil Abdul Munim's call for Pakatan Harapan to review its cooperation with Barisan Nasional in the federal government following BN's electoral understanding with Perikatan Nasional in the Negri Sembilan election. Dr Akmal, who is also Merlimau assemblyman, said Kamil should not single out BN over the arrangement, arguing that similar political cooperation had taken place in previous elections without triggering similar concerns. On Saturday, Kamil urged PH to reassess its political cooperation with BN in the unity government following the cooperation between the coalition led by Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and PN in Negri Sembilan.

items.title: Syed Saddiq pledges 100pct of RM650,959 raised from gratitude run to Muar residents
items.summary: MUAR: Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman concluded his four-day gratitude run today, raising RM650,959.21, more than three times his initial target. The Muar member of parliament said the entire amount would be distributed to eligible recipients in the constituency and would not be used for administrative purposes. He said the money entrusted by the public would not be touched, even to fund the operations or staff expenses of the Muar parliamentary service centre.

items.title: Azalina: Political cooperation must put people first
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno has played down speculation over closer ties with Pas following Barisan Nasional's landslide victory in Johor, saying any future political cooperation must be guided by sincerity and the interests of the people rather than the pursuit of power. Umno information chief Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said BN remained committed to the Unity Government and dismissed suggestions that the coalition was seeking political cooperation merely to consolidate power. Her remarks come amid growing speculation over closer ties between BN and Perikatan Nasional, particularly between Umno and Pas, following the Johor election and recent electoral arrangements involving BN and PN in Negri Sembilan.

items.title: ‘Thanks for the help’: Loke says MCA’s retreat boosts DAP in three Negeri Sembilan seats
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 19 — DAP Secretary-General Anthony Loke today described MCA’s decision not to contest three of its traditional seats in the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election as a move that indirectly benefits DAP by improving its chances of retaining those constituencies. “I would like to thank MCA president Datuk Seri Dr Wee Ka Siong for being so ‘generous’ in helping DAP by not contesting. He also expressed confidence that DAP would retain all 11 state seats it is contesting, contributing to PH’s bid to form the Negeri Sembilan state government.

items.title: Survivors of Indonesia boat sinking rescued after days at sea
items.summary: JAKARTA: Five survivors from a passenger boat that sank off eastern Indonesia, including a seven-year-old girl, have been rescued after floating at sea for three days, a rescue official said on Sunday. Survivors told rescuers they had been part of a group of 25 people earlier reported as missing before becoming separated from the others by heavy winds. "After the ship sank, each of them saved themselves using whatever equipment or makeshift flotation they could find," local search-and-rescue official Muhammad Arif Anwar told AFP.

items.title: Malaysia must prepare now for expected Super El Niño in November
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia must begin preparing now for the possibility of a Super El Niño expected to develop from November, with early action crucial to minimising its impact on lives, livelihoods and the environment. "Previous El Niño episodes had demonstrated that prolonged hot and dry weather could trigger water shortages, forest and peatland fires, transboundary haze, reduced agricultural yields, food security concerns, health risks and disruptions to economic activities. "The expected Super El Niño should therefore be viewed not merely as a seasonal phenomenon but as another reminder that Malaysia must strengthen its climate resilience and disaster preparedness," he said in a statement.

items.title: Kedah royals meet Help University blast survivor Reeya
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Help University blast survivor Reeya Kaur Thandal and her family received a royal gesture of support when the Kedah royal family met them at Istana Kedah here, offering encouragement as the 19-year-old continues her recovery from serious injuries. The meeting on Saturday was attended by the Raja Muda of Kedah Tengku Sarafudin Badlishah Ibni Al Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin, the Raja Puan Muda of Kedah Che Puan Muda Zaheeda Mohamad Ariff, and their children, Tunku Zara Bahiyah and Tunku Sulaiman Badlishah. Earlier on Jan 16, Che Puan Muda Zaheeda visited Reeya at the Tunku Abdul Rahman Neurology Ward in Hospital Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: Hadi calls for Malay-Muslim unity with 'non-extreme' partners including MCA, MIC
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang has urged voters to support a political formula built around cooperation between Pas, Umno, and "non-extreme" non-Muslim parties, including MCA and MIC, as he called for a wider unity movement to "save Malaysia." In a Facebook post today, Abdul Hadi said the approach was based on the principles of unity and cooperation between Malay Muslims, Bumiputera communities and non-Muslims who, he said, did not adopt extreme positions. "This should begin with Pas together with Umno, Barisan Nasional, Malay-Muslim groups and non-Muslim parties that are not extreme, such as MCA, MIC, the Dewan Himpunan Pendukung Pas (DHPP) and others," he said.

items.title: SKDS registrations reach 172,955 companies nationwide
items.summary: LABUAN: A total of 172,955 companies involving 464,797 vehicles have registered under the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) nationwide as of July 18. Of the total, 18,770 companies with 51,679 vehicles were registered under the land transport sector for consumer goods, while 7,241 companies involving 13,557 vehicles were registered under the company-owned land transport sector covering jeeps and pickup trucks. Another 2,116 companies involving 3,433 vehicles were registered under the public land transport sector.

items.title: Wife of Indian activist Wangchuk asks court to move him to private hospital
items.summary: NEW DELHI: Social activist Sonam Wangchuk's wife has approached an Indian court seeking permission to transfer him from a government hospital to a private facility, alleging he was being held in "illegal detention," she said on Sunday. "Despite repeated requests, the hospital has refused to discharge him or allow us to shift him to a private hospital of our choice," Gitanjali Angmo said in a post on social media website X, a day after Wangchuk was moved to state-run Safdarjung Hospital against his wishes by security personnel. On Saturday, he was refusing treatment, hospital authorities had said.

items.title: China's Jingye warns of legal action after nationalisation of British Steel
items.summary: BEIJING: Jingye Steel demanded compensation and threatened the UK government with legal action on Sunday, after London took control of British Steel from the Chinese firm. Jingye, which bought British Steel in 2020, lost control of it last year when the UK government intervened at the struggling firm. The UK government said Thursday it had taken British Steel into full public ownership to "protect the future of steel production in the UK", after taking control from Jingye.

items.title: Activist remanded two days over criminal threat on Facebook
items.summary: The remand order against the 34-year-old man was issued by magistrate Naeilah Nikrul Tarmizi at the magistrate's court here today. Negri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad yesterday said the man surrendered at the Port Dickson district police headquarters after fleeing to Thailand in March. He said police received a report from a man at 6.25pm on March 10, claiming he had been threatened by the suspect on the social media platform.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan polls: Cousins turn political rivals in three-way contest for Klawang
items.summary: JELEBU, July 19 — The campaign trail at Kuala Klawang weekend market here took an interesting turn today when two cousins contesting the Klawang state seat in the Negeri Sembilan State Election crossed paths while engaging with voters. Incumbent Datuk Bakri Sawir of Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Danni Rais of Perikatan Nasional (PN) were seen engaging with visitors and traders at the market as campaigning entered its second day today. The Klawang state seat, which has 13,355 registered voters, will see a three-cornered fight involving Bakri, Danni and Bersatu’s Muhammad Adib Musa.

items.title: Cousins put politics aside during campaign trail encounter
items.summary: That was the spontaneous message from Pakatan Harapan candidate for the Klawang state seat, Datuk Bakri Sawir, to his Perikatan Nasional counterpart Danni Rais when the two cousins crossed paths during campaigning at the Kuala Klawang Sunday Market today. The two exchanged greetings and chatted briefly before Bakri asked Danni to convey his regards to his uncle, Tan Sri Dr Rais Yatim. Bakri said he had no issue greeting his cousin whenever they met during the campaign period, despite representing opposing political camps.

items.title: Russian missiles strike Kyiv killing one, wounding 13
items.summary: KYIV: Russian missiles struck the Ukrainian capital Kyiv and the surrounding region early on Sunday, killing at least one person and wounding 13 others as fires broke out across the city, officials said. Fires broke out at a dormitory, an apartment building and a supermarket, Kyiv Mayor Vitali Klitschko said on the Telegram app. he said.

items.title: Water treatment waste turned into high-value bricks in Penang
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM: Water treatment plant residue, previously classified as scheduled waste, is being transformed into high-value construction materials through an innovative circular economy initiative. The breakthrough follows a collaboration between Alam Aliran Kualiti (M) Sdn Bhd (AAK) and the Penang Water Supply Corporation (PBAPP), which has successfully converted the SW204 scheduled waste into raw material for cement brick production. The initiative makes PBAPP the first water operator in Malaysia to adopt a circular economy model for managing water treatment plant residue, while AAK is the country's first facility to process SW204 into brick material.

items.title: Rosmah thanks supporters, seeks prayers for Najib's recovery [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor has urged the public to pray for a smooth medical procedure for her husband, former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak, who is scheduled to undergo treatment to remove plaque near a blood vessel. Rosmah confirmed the matter in a Facebook post yesterday, expressing appreciation for the concern and support shown by Najib's friends and supporters. It was reported on Friday that Najib would undergo a related procedure next week.

items.title: ‘Do not forget’: Anwar urges Malaysian youth to remember struggle for independence
items.summary: IPOH, July 19 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today urged the current generation to appreciate the country’s history of struggle and understand the meaning of independence that has been achieved through the sacrifices of previous generations. Speaking at the launch of the 2026 National Month and Fly the Jalur Gemilang Campaign (MPBKKJG 2026) here, Anwar said the struggle that demanded the blood, sweat and tears of past independence fighters should teach Malaysians to appreciate the comforts they enjoy today. Also present were Communications Minister Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil, National Unity Minister Datuk Aaron Ago Dagang and Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad.

items.title: Ministry: Nearly 173,000 companies register for subsidised diesel scheme nationwide, covering 465,000 vehicles
items.summary: He said 28,127 companies involving 68,669 vehicles were registered in Sabah, Sarawak and the Federal Territory of Labuan following the expansion of the targeted diesel subsidy mechanism to the three regions on July 1. Of the total, 18,770 companies with 51,679 vehicles were registered under the land transport sector for consumer goods, while 7,241 companies involving 13,557 vehicles were registered under the company-owned land transport sector covering jeeps and pickup trucks. Another 2,116 companies involving 3,433 vehicles were registered under the public land transport sector.

items.title: North Borneo stamps a living archive of Sabah history, says collector
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Efforts to preserve North Borneo stamps, now over a century old, are becoming increasingly crucial as these rare collectibles disappear, along with the historical records of Sabah's early development. The survey uncovered an album containing a six-sen North Borneo stamp featuring Queen Elizabeth II alongside a Dusun woman, as well as a 10-sen stamp depicting logging activities issued between 1954 and 1961. Shari, who has been involved in philately for nearly 40 years, said North Borneo stamps were introduced in 1883 by the British North Borneo Chartered Company and remained in use for about 52 years.

items.title: Johor man loses RM1.1mil in scam involving fake prosecutor, bank officers
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A 63-year-old self-employed man lost RM1.1 million after falling victim to a phone scam involving fraudsters impersonating a prosecutor and Bank Negara Malaysia officers. He was then connected to several "Bank Negara officers", who instructed him to open a new bank account for investigation purposes and transfer his savings into it. Azrul reminded the public that no enforcement agency, prosecutor, court, Bank Negara Malaysia, Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission or government ministry would instruct individuals to open new bank accounts, apply for loans, transfer money or disclose banking details through phone calls.

items.title: MCMC, Health Ministry to launch drone medicine delivery pilot project in Tawau
items.summary: The initiative expands the role of the National Information Dissemination Centre (Nadi) in healthcare delivery. MCMC Nadi Office head Dr Mohd Arif Adenan said the initiative, under the Nadi Smart Services programme, is expected to benefit residents in about 12 villages, particularly those in rural and remote areas who face difficulties obtaining regular medicine supplies. Arif said the initiative reflects MCMC's efforts to transform Nadi from a digital connectivity hub into a community centre offering wider access to essential government and public services.

items.title: Construction work halted after methane gas leak detected
items.summary: MIRI: Construction work at a site along Jalan Perdana, Tudan here was halted today after methane gas was detected leaking from a cylinder pile. A Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department operations centre spokesperson said the Bintulu Hazardous Materials (Hazmat) team was deployed to the scene to carry out further checks. "Initial checks by a Petros contractor revealed that the gas detected was methane, with a 100 per cent Lower Explosive Limit (LEL) reading.

items.title: Fahmi: Be cautious when using AI to generate content featuring Jalur Gemilang
items.summary: IPOH: Communications Minister Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil has reminded Malaysians to ensure the Jalur Gemilang is displayed correctly, especially when using artificial intelligence (AI) to generate content involving the national flag. He said every detail of the Jalur Gemilang, including its 14 stripes, must be represented accurately as it carried significant meaning. The 14 stripes on the Jalur Gemilang are very important," he said.

items.title: Agrobank expands Agro Penjaja-i's reach
items.summary: Azanis Shahila Aman KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Pertanian Malaysia Bhd (Agrobank) has formed a partnership with the Federation of Malay Hawkers and Small Traders Associations Malaysia to expand its Agro Penjaja-i initiative. Agrobank president and group chief executive officer Tengku Datuk Ahmad Badli Shah Raja Hussin said small-time traders operate viable businesses but continue to face challenges in accessing capital, managing finances and adopting digital solutions. The initiative also has 370 Agrobank digital banking enrolments and 311 DuitNow QR activations.

items.title: Kelantan raids foil attempts to smuggle cooking oil into Thailand
items.summary: State Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry enforcement chief Aswadi Jaafar said the latest crackdown under Op Tiris 4.0 (Integrated) resulted in the seizure of more than 20 tonnes of subsidised cooking oil worth RM138,000 in two separate operations in Pasir Mas and Tumpat. Aswadi said investigations showed syndicates no longer relied solely on supplies from Kelantan, but had expanded their network by obtaining subsidised cooking oil from suppliers in other states before stockpiling it near illegal jetties along the border. "A search of the vehicle uncovered 1,040 boxes containing 17,680kg of subsidised cooking oil packets.

items.title: Azalina: Over US$1.37b in 1MDB-linked assets returned by US, more still tied up overseas
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 19 — More than US$1.37 billion in assets linked to the misappropriation of 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) funds have been returned by the United States to Malaysia to date, said Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said. “In addition to the assets that have been returned, a substantial amount of funds and assets linked to 1MDB remain detained, frozen or subject to forfeiture proceedings in the United States and several other foreign jurisdictions,” she said in a written parliamentary reply. On the exact value of the funds and assets that remain detained, frozen or subject to forfeiture proceedings, Azalina said it could not be determined precisely at this time.

items.title: Activist remanded for two days in Seremban after returning from Thailand
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 19 — A 33-year-old activist has reportedly been remanded for two days to assist investigations into alleged threats made on social media in March. Negri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad reportedly said the man was detained to facilitate investigations under Section 506 of the Penal Code and Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998. Police opened the investigation after receiving a police report on March 10 alleging that the suspect had made threatening posts Facebook .

items.title: Electrician loses RM150,000 in WhatsApp investment scam
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, July 19 — An electrician lost RM150,000 after falling victim to a non-existent investment syndicate early this month. Kuala Terengganu police chief ACP Azli Mohd Noor said the 48-year-old victim received a WhatsApp message on July 3 from a woman promoting an investment scheme known as “Centralize International”. He said the suspect persuaded the victim to participate by promising substantial returns within a short period.

items.title: Authorities confirm no one harmed in Melaka shophouse collapse
items.summary: MELAKA, July 19 — The Melaka Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) has confirmed that no one was trapped in the collapse of a two-storey shophouse that was undergoing renovation in Taman Kota Laksamana yesterday, following a search operation and verification with the relevant agencies. Melaka JBPM director Mohd Zaidi Ma’at said the confirmation was based on inspections and search operations conducted by firefighters at the scene, as well as investigations carried out jointly with the police, the Melaka Historic City Council (MBMB) and the building owner. “The fire and rescue team conducted a search at the collapse site and found that no victims were involved in the building collapse.

items.title: Police approve 19 permits as Negeri Sembilan campaigns get under way
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 19 — A total of 19 permit applications for ceramah (political talks) and political campaign activities received since yesterday for the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election have been approved. Negeri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said the applications were approved after meeting all the stipulated requirements. “Overall, the security and public order situation throughout the 16h Negeri Sembilan State Election remains good and under control.

items.title: Medicine from the sky: MCMC and Health Ministry to begin drone deliveries in Tawau
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 19 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), in collaboration with the Health Ministry, will launch a pilot project using drones to deliver medicines in Tawau, Sabah, around September or October, expanding the role of the National Information Dissemination Centre (NADI) in healthcare delivery. MCMC NADI Office head Dr Mohd Arif Adenan said the initiative, under the NADI Smart Services programme, is expected to benefit residents in about 12 villages, particularly those in rural and remote areas who face difficulties obtaining regular medicine supplies. Mohd Arif said MCMC would consider expanding the initiative to other areas with similar needs if the pilot project achieves its intended objectives.

items.title: Rescuers halt search at wrecked Melaka shophouse amid fears of further collapse
items.summary: MELAKA, July 19 — The Melaka Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) has suspended search and rescue (SAR) operations at the site of a collapsed two-storey shophouse in Taman Kota Laksamana here yesterday until the status of all workers at the premises is confirmed, while taking into account the risk posed by the unstable structure. Melaka Tengah Fire and Rescue Station chief Senior Fire Superintendent II K. Karuppannan said the department would not risk the safety of its personnel by entering the collapsed structure until it is confirmed whether anyone remains trapped. The collapsed two-storey shophouse at Taman Kota Laksamana in Melaka, July 18, 2026.

items.title: Najib to undergo procedure to remove plaque near blood vessel, says Rosmah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 19 — Former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak is scheduled to undergo a medical procedure to remove plaque located near a blood vessel, his wife Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor said. In a Facebook post last night, Rosmah thanked those who had contacted them to ask about Najib’s condition and offered prayers and words of encouragement. Rosmah also expressed her gratitude for the support shown towards her husband.

items.title: Man dies after tree falls on two cars along Jalan Tun Razak in KL
items.summary: Dang Wangi police chief ACP Sazalee Adam said the victim suffered injuries to both arms and internal bleeding in the chest. “The victim died while receiving treatment at Kuala Lumpur Hospital (HKL) at 4am today,” he said in a statement. Sazalee said all of them are receiving treatment at HKL, and physical examinations found no external injuries.

items.title: Activist wanted over alleged Facebook threats returns from Thailand and surrenders
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 19 — An activist suspected of making criminal threats via Facebook surrendered at the Port Dickson district police headquarters (IPD) yesterday after it was believed that he had fled to Thailand since March. Negeri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said the 33-year-old local man turned himself in at about 12.45pm after being in the neighbouring country for more than four months. “Investigations found that the police received a report from a man at 6.25pm, on March 10, after allegedly being threatened on the social media site by the suspect.

items.title: Gerakan sacks Tang Jay Son for contesting in Rahang on Bersatu ticket
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 19 — Gerakan has expelled Tang Jay Son from the party with immediate effect after he contested under the Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) ticket in the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election. Gerakan secretary-general Wong Chia Zhen said in a statement yesterday that Tang’s action constituted a serious breach of party discipline as it was contrary to the principle of loyalty to the party. “Gerakan wishes to stress that every member is responsible for complying with the party constitution, respecting organisational decisions, and upholding party discipline and integrity at all times.

items.title: Small firms and local contractors to receive 300-litre monthly diesel quota through fleet cards
items.summary: Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said this flexibility in assistance is granted specifically for diesel vehicles registered under the names of partnership companies or sole proprietorships nationwide for their business use. They use those vehicles for their small business needs,” he said. He was met by reporters after officiating the Madani Foster Village Project at Kampung Sekalong, Menumbok, here yesterday.

items.title: Give Pakatan a stronger mandate to keep Negeri Sembilan on course, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 19 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) Chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has appealed for the consideration and trust of the people of Negeri Sembilan to grant a stronger mandate to PH in the upcoming State Election (PRN). The Election Commission (EC) informed that a total of 103 candidates have been confirmed to contest in the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election to vie for 36 State Legislative Assembly (DUN) seats, after the nomination process concluded yesterday. PH dominates the contest by fielding 36 candidates, including Aminuddin in the Linggi state seat, followed by Barisan Nasional (BN) with 25 candidates, Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) with 24 candidates, and Perikatan Nasional (PN) with 11 candidates.

items.title: From an idea to 800 sterilisations: How Catsoterra is helping to curb the stray cat population in Penang
items.summary: It started out as Cats of Penang in April last year and was formally established as Catsoterra Berhad in November. To date, it has facilitated the sterilisation of almost 800 cats across Penang and subsidised more than RM50,000 worth of TNR services (as of July 8). Explaining the Catsoterra system, he said volunteers who join the platform pay an annual RM60 membership fee.

items.title: How ordinary Penangites are taking on the stray cat population, one sterilisation at a time
items.summary: Motivated by her love for cats, Fouziah has also taken in 40 cats and rented a space to house them. The assistant manager spends about RM2,500 a month on feeding and taking care of the 40 cats and the stray cats. Catsoterra, which was set up in Penang only last year, is a volunteer-driven animal welfare organisation that helps facilitate the sterilisation of stray cats in Penang.

items.title: PM Anwar calls on the people to share views via his AI avatar (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 18 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has invited the people, especially the young, to share their views, concerns and aspirations directly through his avatar to ensure that the country’s development continues to be based on the voices and wishes of the people. “So, I really hope that you can share your views, concerns and aspirations directly through this avatar because I am convinced that the country will only improve if it is based on the voices and wishes of the people,” he said. The Prime Minister said Malaysia was built by the aspirations, qualities and strengths of its people of various races and cultures, and invited all the people to participate in the campaign to create a fresh discourse for the country and the people.

items.title: Fractured alliances, multi-cornered fights shape Negeri Sembilan state election after nominations close
items.summary: A total of 103 candidates will contest all 36 state seats, with the Election Commission (EC) confirming 11 straight fights, 21 three-cornered contests, two four-cornered contests and two five-cornered battles. Another highlight battle is in Rantau, where Umno deputy president Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan (BN) will defend his seat agaisnt PKR’s Dr Azizul Hakim Mahdi (PH). Attention will definitely be on Linggi, where outgoing Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun (PH) is seeking a fresh mandate after moving from his long-held Sikamat seat, to face a strong BN opponent Datuk Mohd Faizal Ramli, and Bersatu’s Datuk Zamri Md Said.

items.title: Flash floods swamp Petaling Jaya after heavy rain (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 18 — Several areas in Petaling Jaya were hit by flash floods, including the 223 Food Court after heavy rain occurred in the Klang Valley this afternoon. Selangor Fire and Rescue Operations Division assistant director Ashrul Riezal Asbar said a team from Petaling Jaya Fire and Rescue Station arrived at the flooded area at 223 Food Court about 20 minutes after they received a call at 4.35 pm to find that the water level was almost two metres high. — Bernama Banjir kilat sekitar Mid Valley, Petaling Jaya, Kelana Jaya dan beberapa lokasi sekitar KL & Selangor.

items.title: Too much, not too little: Deputy premier Dr Sim says overeating is now Sarawak’s bigger health challenge
items.summary: KUCHING, July 18 — Public health policies should place greater emphasis on tackling overnutrition, as it is a major contributor to the rising incidence of diabetes among Sarawakians, said Deputy Premier Datuk Amar Dr Sim Kui Hian. Dr Sim said while undernutrition remains an issue, it affects only a small percentage of the population compared with overnutrition, which now requires greater attention. Dr Sim said the prevalence of diabetes in Sarawak is between 17 and 18 per cent, compared with about 21 per cent nationwide.

items.title: Roofs torn off as fierce winds lash Sibu Jaya, no injuries reported
items.summary: SIBU, July 18 —Several houses in Sibu Jaya sustained roof damage after strong winds accompanied by a heavy downpour swept through the area at about 3pm today. Most of the affected homeowners were away when the storm struck, leaving neighbours to witness roofs being ripped off by the powerful winds. A resident, who declined to be named, said the weather deteriorated rapidly before strong winds battered the neighbourhood.

items.title: Muhyiddin claims Bersatu is Parliament’s only true Opposition amid shifting alliances
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 18 — Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin has claimed Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia is now the country’s only “standalone” Opposition party, arguing that it must remain independent as the country’s sole credible check on the government amid shifting political alliances. “In fact, the challenges are even greater now because Bersatu is the only standalone Opposition party in the Malaysian Parliament,” he wrote. Muhyiddin argued that Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Barisan Nasional (BN) were both part of the federal government, while Perikatan Nasional’s (PN) political cooperation with BN had, in his view, effectively turned the coalition into a supporter of the government.

items.title: Three Form One boys in Kangar under police investigation over alleged bullying that left classmate with fractured ankle
items.summary: KANGAR, July 18 — Three Form One students at a secondary school here have been remanded for two days from today to assist investigations into the alleged bullying of a schoolmate that left him with a fractured left ankle. Kangar police chief ACP Yusharifuddin Mohd Yusop said police received a report on the incident at 11.27 pm on Thursday. He said the victim’s 39-year-old mother had earlier been informed by his class teacher at 12.28 pm that her son, also a Form One student, had slipped while entering the classroom and was unable to walk.

items.title: Busker’s bid for Sikamat ends before it begins after election paperwork snag
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 18 — Government retiree Bujang Abu’s aspiration to contest as an independent candidate for the Sikamat state seat in the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election fell through after documents required by the Election Commission (EC) were found to be incomplete. “During the nomination process, I was informed that I had not submitted the expenditure statement for the 2023 state election. He previously contested the Sikamat state seat in the 2013 General Election (GE) and the 2023 State Election but was unsuccessful on both occasions.

items.title: New Seremban orthopaedic centre launching this year aims to cut treatment costs by up to 50 per cent
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 18 — Bank Rakyat has provided financing totalling RM1.42 million to Limbcraft Innovation Sdn Bhd to develop an affordable orthopaedic centre here that is expected to offer treatment and surgery costs up to 50 per cent lower, depending on the type of procedure. Entrepreneur and Cooperative Development Minister Steven Sim said the financing was channelled for the renovation of the premises as well as the purchase of machinery and equipment for Axis Orthopaedic Specialist Centre & Ambulatory Care Centre, which is expected to be launched this year. He said the centre would be equipped with an operation theatre, day treatment wards and robotic rehabilitation facilities, while the financing was provided at a low profit rate of four per cent per annum.

items.title: MACC steps in over KWAP’s RM200m investment loss linked to Indonesia’s eFishery
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 18 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has launched an investigation into the RM200 million investment loss suffered by the Retirement Fund (Incorporated) (KWAP) involving Indonesian aquaculture technology company eFishery. MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said an investigation team from the commission’s headquarters was formed yesterday to conduct a comprehensive review and assessment of the matter. The ministry said a consortium of investors, including KWAP, had taken firm action, including pursuing legal proceedings, efforts to recover funds, conducting internal governance reviews and strengthening controls to protect investment interests.
